decks.notes: The deck focuses on using disruptive creatures and spells to slow down the opponent while creating a fast clock with aggressive creatures. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Skyclave Apparition. The Aether Vial helps cheat creatures into play quickly, and equipment like Swords to Plowshares and Stoneforge Mystic provide additional removal and utility. The deck also utilizes Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion for extra value and flexibility. Overall, the strategy is to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent.

The deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo quickly to win the game. It aims to use cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Lion's Eye Diamond to generate mana efficiently and cast Doomsday as early as possible. Thassa's Oracle is the win condition once Doomsday resolves, with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo. The deck also includes cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to help dig for combo pieces and interaction. The mana base is designed to support the combo and ensure the deck can consistently execute its game plan.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures like White Orchid Phantom, Amped Raptor, and Ocelot Pride to pressure the opponent while using cards like Goblin Bombardment and Swords to Plowshares for removal. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts the opponent's spells while Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional damage and evasion. The mana base is strong with a mix of fetch lands, dual lands, and utility lands like Karakas and Wasteland to ensure consistency and disruption. Overall, this deck aims to win the game swiftly through aggressive creature-based strategies.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's plans while applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent. Using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board, it aims to maintain tempo and advantage. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and versatility, while Kaito, Bane of Nightmares puts pressure on the opponent's hand. Key spells like Back to Basics and Toxic Deluge help to control the board and lock down the opponent's resources. Mystic Sanctuary can be used to recur important spells and maintain consistency in the late game.

The main concept of this deck is to use artifacts to control and lock down the opponent's board, while also generating mana quickly with cards like Grim Monolith and Urza's lands. Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms provide additional control elements, while cards like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus disrupt the opponent's game plan. The win condition revolves around using cards like The One Ring and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn to finish off the opponent once the board is locked down. The deck aims to create a controlling and prison-like environment for the opponent to struggle against.

This deck focuses on aggressively disrupting the opponent's game plan while maintaining a steady tempo and pressure. It utilizes countermagic and efficient removal spells to control the board and protect its threats. The deck's key strength lies in its ability to quickly develop a dominant board presence with evasive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler, backed up by card advantage engines like Mishra's Bauble and Brainstorm. The deck aims to close out the game swiftly by overwhelming the opponent with a combination of burn spells and powerful creatures.

The main idea of this UWx Control Legacy deck is to maintain control of the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and generating card advantage through card draw and manipulation. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying its own threats like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. It utilizes powerful card selection tools like Brainstorm and Ponder, along with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending. The mana base is optimized to support the deck's color requirements and includes utility lands like Mystic Sanctuary and Wasteland.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The key strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage and eventually overpowering them with powerful threats like Teferi, Time Raveler. The deck uses a mix of versatile removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending, along with counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation to control the game. Card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder help to find key cards and maintain card advantage, while powerful finishers like Lórien Revealed and Wrath of the Skies close out the game.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize cards like Throes of Chaos and Creative Technique to cheat out powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Apex Devastator. The deck also aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Tibalt's Trickery while ramping quickly with mana-producing lands like Ancient Tomb and City of Traitors. The combo potential with cards like Call Forth the Tempest and Violent Outburst allows for explosive plays, while also incorporating elements of surprise and unpredictability with cards like Tinder Farm and Boseiju, Who Endures.

The main concept of this UR Tempo deck is to quickly establish board control and tempo advantage through efficient spellcasting and aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards like Mishra's Bauble and Dragon's Rage Channeler to generate card advantage and fuel Murktide Regent, the deck aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Force of Will and Wasteland while dealing damage with Lightning Bolt and other direct damage spells. The deck seeks to maintain pressure and adapt to opponent's strategies through a mix of disruption, card draw, and board presence.

The deck focuses on creating a lock on the board through the use of artifacts and powerful planeswalkers like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to restrict the opponent's options and slow down their game plan. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Candelabra of Tawnos further solidifies the control aspect of the deck, while cards like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provide a powerful win condition. The deck synergizes well with artifacts and aims to dominate the board through its prison-like strategies.

The main concept of this deck is to establish a control-based strategy through the use of artifacts, focusing on creating a prison-like board state that restricts the opponent's options. The deck utilizes powerful artifacts such as Urza's Saga, Candelabra of Tawnos, The One Ring, and Planar Nexus to control the game. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Grim Monolith, and Trinisphere further solidifies the control aspect of the deck. Overall, the goal is to lock down the opponent's resources and win through incremental advantage gained from powerful artifacts and control elements.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while quickly putting pressure on them with aggressive creatures. Using disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares, along with disruption from Aether Vial and Karakas, the deck aims to control the game tempo. The creatures in the deck, such as Stoneforge Mystic and Skyclave Apparition, provide additional disruption and threats on the board. By combining disruption with aggressive creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory through a combination of tempo advantage and efficient creature combat.

The main concept of this deck is to lock down the opponent using a combination of artifacts and control elements. By utilizing powerful artifacts such as Ugin, Eye of the Storm, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and limit their options. With cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and The One Ring to generate mana and Urza's Saga to tutor for key pieces, the deck can quickly establish a strong board presence while preventing the opponent from mounting a successful offense.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Griselbrand, and then reanimate them using spells like Animate Dead and Reanimate. The deck also includes cards like Faithless Looting and Unmask to help with card selection and disruption. This combo-heavy strategy aims to overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures and powerful abilities while controlling the board with cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize. The deck uses a mix of fast mana like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal along with fetch lands to quickly set up its game plan.

The main theme of this Mardu Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creature plays and disruptive spells. By playing powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls alongside cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to maintain constant pressure and control the game. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Voice of Victory also provide added versatility and reach, allowing for different lines of play to secure victory.
